title: inflation / cpi / seasonality | Research Paper | Pricing in the New Year: Why Inflation Behaves Differently a...
summary: Boston Fed researchers analyze three factors driving elevated January inflation: residual seasonality in CPI data, higher frequency of price changes at the start of the year (over 28% of prices change from December to January), and compositional effects where sectors with the strongest tendency to change prices in January experienced particularly high inflation from 2023-2025. The research uses BLS price microdata and covers the 1998-2025 period.
